text.title: rust-tokio-async Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-tokio-async as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Calling blocking syscalls (std::fs::read, reqwest::blocking, image processing) directly from an async function.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Blocks the Tokio worker thread for the duration of the syscall. On a 4-thread runtime, 4 simultaneous blocking calls starve all other async tasks until they complete.

failure-mode.description: Calling tokio::runtime::Handle::block_on or Runtime::block_on from within an async context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Panics with "cannot block the current thread from within a runtime" — a cryptic error that appears in tests. Test functions must use #[tokio::test] or block_on in a sync context only.

failure-mode.description: Spawning every task with tokio::spawn without awaiting or aborting the returned JoinHandle.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Orphaned handles keep tasks alive indefinitely. Task panics and errors are silently discarded. Prefer try_join! or JoinSet for structured concurrency; use spawn only for true fire-and-forget background tasks.

failure-mode.description: Using std::sync::Mutex and holding the guard across an .await point.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The Mutex guard is not Send. The compiler rejects this with a wall of trait-bound errors, but the intent is to block the async thread — which must use tokio::sync::Mutex or restructure to not hold the lock across yield points.

failure-mode.description: Placing a non-cancel-safe future (DB transaction, multi-step file write) on a tokio::select! branch.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When a select! branch is not selected, the future is dropped at the next .await point, leaving partial state. Only cancel-safe primitives (recv, sleep, AsyncRead) belong on select branches.
